title: Today's Markets Brief: Your portfolio can’t afford the real bill for the Iran war
summary: A look at the hidden costs of the Iran war and its impact on your investments

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your markets brief. I am looking at some fresh signals that have me thinking about the bigger picture in finance. The headlines are buzzing with news about the Iran war and its economic implications. Let's dive in.

First up, the Pentagon claims the conflict in Iran has cost $29 billion so far. But according to a report from MarketWatch, the true cost is much higher. The real bill is fueling inflation and could have a significant impact on your portfolio. My read is that this hidden cost could be a major factor in the markets right now. It's a bit like finding out your restaurant bill has an extra charge you didn't see - it's not fun, but you've got to pay it.

Next, I'm looking at Intuit's stock performance. It seems the company's stock is underperforming the S&P 500. A report from Barchart has me wondering if there's more to the story. What's behind this underperformance, and what does it mean for investors? It's always interesting to see which companies are lagging behind and why.

And finally, I've been keeping an eye on other market movers, trying to connect the dots between these news items. The market can be like a game of chess - you've got to think a few moves ahead. I'm not calling any shots just yet, but I'm definitely watching.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
